message.content:
你是獨立驗證者（與發現此問題的 reviewer 不同 context）。盡力反駁以下 finding 是真問題——它可能是誤報。
若你能證明它不是問題（誤讀程式碼 / 已被別處處理 / 不在執行路徑），判 isReal=false。若反駁失敗，判 isReal=true。
Finding：{"severity":"medium","where":".claude_temp/respin_and_freegame.json vs P1-design-spec.md §3.1 P-3 / EnterRespinState.ts:84-122","problem":"設計規格 P-3 假設 RespinLockedPos「只含 col1-4」，但真實回包逐筆解碼證實 col0/5 也在 RespinLockedPos 內（Round0 sub0=[0,1,2,3,6,8,9,10,13,14,15,16,20,21,22,23]，其中 0-3=col0、20-23=col5，整列 8 格全鎖）。已 commit 的 EnterRespinState.HandleReel 仍把全部 entry（含 col0/5）merge 進 m_respinLockedSet 並對全集合重建靜態圖；col0/5 既由 SetColumnLock 整列鎖定、又被 EffectPlate 疊靜態張 → 視覺上雙重鎖定/可能蓋圖錯亂。這正是未 commit 的 EXCLUDE_LOCK_COLUMNS_FROM_STATIC=true 要修的問題。","fix":"未 commit 的 EXCLUDE_LOCK_COLUMNS_FROM_STATIC 開關處理方向正確（只跳過 col0/5 的靜態張重建，不過濾 merge），且 AssertLockedConsistency 的 expected 聯集與 actual merge 都含 col0/5 故仍一致。但此修正目前停在 working tree 未提交，commit f2f739e 的版本在 col0/5 會疊冗餘靜態張。建議：(1) 盡快提交該開關修正；(2) 在 spec §3.1 P-3 更正「只含 col1-4」為「含 col0/5 整列」，避免後續維護者依錯誤前提；(3) 執行期確認 col0/5 跳過靜態張後、停輪畫面仍正確顯示鎖定符號（依賴 SetColumnLock 讓 reel 不轉留存原符號，屬 framework 行為）。","lens":"correctness"}
審查目標：P1 Respin 雙層索引重構（含 FeatureWheel→FreeGame 真實回包驗證的前置重構）。

設計規格：.claude-loop/artifacts/P1-design-spec.md（對照 §5 模組職責、§7 BC-x、§8 因果鏈）。
真實 server 回包：.claude_temp/respin_and_freegame.json（14 局：Round[0]=MG 2 plate 觸發 FG、Round[1..13]=FG 內含 respin、retrigger）。

審查範圍 = `git diff bab1dbd~2 HEAD` 的核心變更 + 未 commit 變更（`git diff HEAD` + 未追蹤 tests/RespinPlateLogic.test.ts）。

核心檔（自行用 git/Read/Grep 探索，勿逐行窮舉清單）：
- assets/Script/Respin/RespinPlateLogic.ts（純函式，BC-1/2/3 對應 tests/RespinPlateLogic.test.ts）
- assets/Script/GameView.ts（雙層游標 m_curSubPlateIndex、旗標 getter 化、m_respinLockedSet）
- assets/Script/GameState/{CheckState,EnterRespinState,LeaveRespinState,SpinState,RoundEndState,UnshowPrepareState,ScatterShowState,AwardState}.ts
- assets/Script/{RecoverSpinAck,EffectPlate,SlotReels}.ts

重點關注（不限於此）：
1. 因果鏈完整性——雙層化後 round 級資料 per-手重讀是否有遺漏點（規格 §6 矩陣外的漏網）。
2. 真實資料牴觸規格 P-3：RespinLockedPos 實含 col0/5（規格原假設只 col1-4）。EnterRespinState 的 EXCLUDE_LOCK_COLUMNS_FROM_STATIC 開關處理是否正確、有無雙重鎖定/顯示錯亂。
3. ENTER_RESPIN→SPIN 繞過 ROUND_END 的 V-1 風險（framework roundController 放行）是否在碼上有兜底或隱患。
4. getter 投影（IsGoingToFree/IsGoingToRespinGame）在 FG 內 respin（Round[1..13]）情境語意是否正確。

驗證基線：tests/RespinPlateLogic.test.ts 30/30 pass；TS 5.4.5 下 assets/Script 0 錯誤（基線 10）。
用 Read/Grep 實際查證程式碼，不要憑空判斷。

attachment.content:
- huashu-slides: AI演示文稿全流程制作：内容结构化→设计选型→AI插画/HTML构建→PPTX导出。17种实战验证的视觉风格（漫画/极简/数据叙事/国风），可编辑HTML与全AI视觉两条路径自由选择。当用户提到"做PPT"、"制作幻灯片"、"演示文稿"、"Keynote"、"slides"、"课件"、"汇报材料"、"路演"时使用。
- memory-to-skill: 當使用者要求「檢視 session 資料夾並把內容整理成 skill」、需要從過去對話中抽取可重用模式、或要把剛剛完成的工作經驗固化成 skill 時使用
- ms-agent-scheduled-prompts: 當需要為 LLM agent bridge（Telegram bot、Slack bot、CLI UI）做延遲 / 常態性 prompt 觸發、排程 bridge 指令（如每天凌晨 /restart）、或要在 agent 回覆裡嵌 token 讓使用者自助建排程時使用
- ms-agent-text-token-signaling: 當 LLM agent 需要觸發 host 端動作（傳檔、開網址、執行指令）但協定不允許 agent 端發起自訂 RPC、或想在 streaming 文字回覆中加上 side effect 又不破壞聊天 UX 時使用
- ms-kiro-strreplace-silent-fail: 當用 Kiro 的 strReplace 或 fs-edit 類工具改檔、tool 回報 Successfully replaced N occurrences 但事後 grep / read 發現磁碟內容沒變、或同一個檔案連續發幾個 strReplace 只有部分生效時使用
- ms-portable-skill-authoring: 當寫 skill 會被多個 agent CLI（Kiro / Codex / Claude）或多台機器共用、要避免把 F:\AI\AIMemory 或 ~/.kiro 這類絕對路徑寫死在 SKILL.md、或要讓 bridge 把環境差異注入成變數讓同一份 SKILL.md 跨機器重用時使用
- ms-streaming-token-pipeline: Use when a Telegram/Slack bot streams text via mid-turn edits plus a final render. Both paths must share one transform, or ASK buttons drop, half-tokens leak, and rate-limit recovery overwrites it.
- ms-telegram-ask-button-protocol: Use when emitting <<ASK:...>> for Telegram bridge buttons, or when an ASK token renders verbatim. Label soft cap 20 chars; >20 SHALL use numbered body list with short labels. See SKILL.md for limits.
- ms-telegram-bot-rate-limit-survival: 當 Telegram bot 對使用者 chat 串 streaming edit 或連發多則訊息、遇到 429 Too Many Requests / retry_after 愈累積愈長（60s→183s）、grammY autoRetry 超過 maxDelaySeconds 拋錯、或需要設計「限流期間凍結 UI + 限流結束後自動補發」時使用
- ms-windows-shell-exit-code-false-positive: 當在 Windows PowerShell 或 cmd.exe 中跑 git clone、pip install、npm install、winget 等命令時，明明工作完成卻收到 exit code 1、stderr 有進度條或 notice、shell tool 回報失敗時使用
- ms-windows-shell-interactive-prompt: 當在 Windows PowerShell 或 cmd.exe 中執行讀檔、列目錄、搜尋指令（Get-Content、type、cat、more）導致 tool call 卡住不回、或在 Kiro agent 與 ACP bridge 裡看到 PowerShell 的互動式參數詢問（例如 Path 參數提示）時使用
- skill-creator: Create or update AgentSkills. Use when designing, structuring, or packaging skills with scripts, references, and assets.
- uk-slot-extrabet: UK 老虎機 ExtraBet（額外押注/加倍投注）子系統指南。建立、修改、除錯 ExtraBet 功能，或處理 unshow/replay 還原、單倍率單步驟 / 多倍率兩步驟跳窗變體、多倍率 type 時觸發。涵蓋 framework ExtraBetComponent / extraBetController、專案 ExtraBetWindow 跳窗、ExtraBetEffectManager 動畫、SlotReels 假轉輪整合、GameView 整合多方關係。
- uk-slot-fake-reel-manager: FakeReelManager 假轉輪帶建立、修改、檢查或擴充
- uk-slot-multilang-sync: 把規格 xlsx 的多國語言文字批次同步進 uk 線上老虎機框架各語系的 gameStrings.xml。當使用者要在老虎機專案新增/更新多國語言字串、提到 「gameStrings.xml」「多國語言」「多語系文字」「i18n 字串」「把 xlsx 的翻譯填進去」、 或要為某個 Key 一次寫入多國(常見 26 國)文字時使用。僅適用線上部門 uk 框架 (plist 格式 gameStrings.xml、每語系一目錄、欄標題括號內為語系代碼); 商用部門或其他文字格式不適用。
- uk-slot-state-machine: UK 老虎機狀態機設定，新增、移除或修改 SetStateMachine() 遊戲狀態時觸發。
- vc-kiro-delegate: 將實作任務委派給 Kiro CLI（headless mode + claude-opus-4.6）執行，把主 agent 的 Claude Max 5x 配額（5 小時 message cap）保留給協調與決策，把實作丟給 Kiro 的不限量訂閱算力。Claude Code 負責預估、規劃、light review；Kiro 負責具體實作與第一輪 self-review。當用戶說「用 kiro 做」「交給 kiro」「省 token」「叫 kiro 寫」時務必使用此 skill；當主 agent 自己評估某個任務「預估會花 2k+ tokens」時也要主動委派 — 例如新增函式、寫測試、重構函式、實作模組。即使用戶沒明說「kiro」，只要預估產出規模 ≥2k tokens，就主動委派並在訊息中告知預估值。
- writing-skills: Use when creating new skills, editing existing skills, or verifying skills work before deployment
- dev:init-claude: dev:init-claude — 開發設計閉環初始化
- sc:analyze: Comprehensive code analysis across quality, security, performance, and architecture domains
- sc:brainstorm: Interactive requirements discovery through Socratic dialogue and systematic exploration
- sc:build: Build, compile, and package projects with intelligent error handling and optimization
- sc:business-panel: /sc:business-panel - Business Panel Analysis System
- sc:cleanup: Systematically clean up code, remove dead code, and optimize project structure
- sc:design: Design system architecture, APIs, and component interfaces with comprehensive specifications
- sc:document: Generate focused documentation for components, functions, APIs, and features
- sc:estimate: Provide development estimates for tasks, features, or projects with intelligent analysis
- sc:explain: Provide clear explanations of code, concepts, and system behavior with educational clarity
- sc:git: Git operations with intelligent commit messages and workflow optimization
- sc:help: List all available /sc commands and their functionality
- sc:implement: Feature and code implementation with intelligent persona activation and MCP integration
- sc:improve: Apply systematic improvements to code quality, performance, and maintainability
- sc:index: Generate comprehensive project documentation and knowledge base with intelligent organization
- sc:load: Session lifecycle management with Serena MCP integration for project context loading
- sc:reflect: Task reflection and validation using Serena MCP analysis capabilities
- sc:save: Session lifecycle management with Serena MCP integration for session context persistence
- sc:select-tool: Intelligent MCP tool selection based on complexity scoring and operation analysis
- sc:spawn: Meta-system task orchestration with intelligent breakdown and delegation
- sc:spec-panel: Multi-expert specification review and improvement using renowned specification and software engineering experts
- sc:task: Execute complex tasks with intelligent workflow management and delegation
- sc:test: Execute tests with coverage analysis and automated quality reporting
- sc:troubleshoot: Diagnose and resolve issues in code, builds, deployments, and system behavior
- sc:workflow: Generate structured implementation workflows from PRDs and feature requirements
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dev-design: 架構設計 workflow（取代閉環 Phase 1+1b）：多方案 judge-panel → 對抗驗證砍缺陷 → 設計規格（含 BC-x）。承重核（因果鏈+事實求證）注入每個 agent。
- dev-prd: PRD / 需求探索 workflow（新能力，原方法論無 PRD 階段）：多角度需求探索（problem/user/scope）→ 塑形候選（lean/complete）→ 對抗挑戰 → PRD 文件。事實求證確保需求前提不是憑空假設。
- dev-review: 品質+安全審查 workflow（取代閉環 Phase 3）：parallel 多視角審查（correctness/security/repro lens）→ 對抗驗證 findings。異源 skeptic 打破同源自審天花板（#007）。
- dev-verify: 跨產出物自證 workflow（取代閉環 Phase 5，可選）：可枚舉項用 adversarial-verify；反向遍歷（找死碼/未實作）用輕量 verifier agent（這是 adversarial-verify 結構上蓋不到的缺口）。
- claude-md-management:revise-claude-md: Update CLAUDE.md with learnings from this session
- claude-mem:do: Execute a plan using subagents for implementation
- claude-mem:make-plan: Create an implementation plan with documentation discovery
- codex:rescue: Delegate investigation, an explicit fix request, or follow-up rescue work to the Codex rescue subagent
- codex:setup: Check whether the local Codex CLI is ready and optionally toggle the stop-time review gate
- webwright:craft: Craft a reusable Webwright CLI tool by parameterizing a web task.
- webwright:run: Run a one-shot web task with the Webwright Playwright workflow.
- claude-md-management:claude-md-improver: Audit and improve CLAUDE.md files in repositories. Use when user asks to check, audit, update, improve, or fix CLAUDE.md files. Scans for all CLAUDE.md files, evaluates quality against templates, outputs quality report, then makes targeted updates. Also use when the user mentions "CLAUDE.md maintenance" or "project memory optimization".
- superpowers:brainstorming: You MUST use this before any creative work - creating features, building components, adding functionality, or modifying behavior. Explores user intent, requirements and design before implementation.
- superpowers:dispatching-parallel-agents: Use when facing 2+ independent tasks that can be worked on without shared state or sequential dependencies
- superpowers:executing-plans: Use when you have a written implementation plan to execute in a separate session with review checkpoints
- superpowers:finishing-a-development-branch: Use when implementation is complete, all tests pass, and you need to decide how to integrate the work - guides completion of development work by presenting structured options for merge, PR, or cleanup
- superpowers:receiving-code-review: Use when receiving code review feedback, before implementing suggestions, especially if feedback seems unclear or technically questionable - requires technical rigor and verification, not performative agreement or blind implementation
- superpowers:requesting-code-review: Use when completing tasks, implementing major features, or before merging to verify work meets requirements
- superpowers:subagent-driven-development: Use when executing implementation plans with independent tasks in the current session
- superpowers:systematic-debugging: Use when encountering any bug, test failure, or unexpected behavior, before proposing fixes
- superpowers:test-driven-development: Use when implementing any feature or bugfix, before writing implementation code
- superpowers:using-git-worktrees: Use when starting feature work that needs isolation from current workspace or before executing implementation plans - ensures an isolated workspace exists via native tools or git worktree fallback
- superpowers:using-superpowers: Use when starting any conversation - establishes how to find and use skills, requiring Skill tool invocation before ANY response including clarifying questions
- superpowers:verification-before-completion: Use when about to claim work is complete, fixed, or passing, before committing or creating PRs - requires running verification commands and confirming output before making any success claims; evidence before assertions always
- superpowers:writing-plans: Use when you have a spec or requirements for a multi-step task, before touching code
- superpowers:writing-skills: Use when creating new skills, editing existing skills, or verifying skills work before deployment
- claude-mem:mem-search: Search claude-mem's persistent cross-session memory database. Use when user asks "did we already solve this?", "how did we do X last time?", or needs work from previous sessions.
- telegram:access: Manage Telegram channel access — approve pairings, edit allowlists, set DM/group policy. Use when the user asks to pair, approve someone, check who's allowed, or change policy for the Telegram channel.
- telegram:configure: Set up the Telegram channel — save the bot token and review access policy. Use when the user pastes a Telegram bot token, asks to configure Telegram, asks "how do I set this up" or "who can reach me," or wants to check channel status.
- codex:codex-cli-runtime: Internal helper contract for calling the codex-companion runtime from Claude Code
- codex:codex-result-handling: Internal guidance for presenting Codex helper output back to the user
- codex:gpt-5-4-prompting: Internal guidance for composing Codex and GPT-5.4 prompts for coding, review, diagnosis, and research tasks inside the Codex Claude Code plugin
- webwright:webwright: Solve a user-specified web task code-as-action style by driving a local Playwright browser through one bash command at a time, saving screenshots and an action log into `final_runs/run_<id>/`, and visually verifying the result. Use when the user asks to automate a web task (search, filter, form-fill, multi-step flow, data extraction) and wants reusable scripts plus screenshot evidence rather than a one-shot answer.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: I'll verify this finding by examining the actual code and design spec. Let me start by reading the relevant files.

input.command: git log --oneline -5 && echo "---DIFF HEAD---" && git diff HEAD --stat
input.description: Show recent commits and uncommitted changes

f2f739e feat: Respin 雙層索引重構 — 結構化判斷旗標 + plate 內循環
bab1dbd feat: 更新 RespinLockedPos 屬性以支持新功能
d81f1e9 補上X倍的時候要有數字在右下角
78b7978 選擇框製作

content.text: Now let me look at the key claim: that the finding describes the EXCLUDE_LOCK_COLUMNS_FROM_STATIC change as "未 commit" (uncommitted). Let me check the actual git state of this change.

input.description: Check committed f2f739e version for the exclude switch

content.content:

/usr/bin/bash: line 3: Select-String: command not found

toolUseResult:
Error: Exit code 127
/usr/bin/bash: line 3: Select-String: command not found

content.text: The finding's core factual claim is confirmed: the EXCLUDE_LOCK_COLUMNS_FROM_STATIC switch is indeed uncommitted (in working tree only). Let me verify what the committed f2f739e version actually had.

input.command: git show f2f739e:assets/Script/GameState/EnterRespinState.ts | grep -nE "EXCLUDE_LOCK|RespinLockColumns|含 col|只含 col|forEach"

content.text: Confirmed. The committed f2f739e version does NOT have the exclude switch, and its loop runs `CreateSymbolStaticNode` for ALL entries including col0/5. Now let me verify the real server data claim — that RespinLockedPos actually contains col0/5.
`RespinLockColumns = [0, 5]`. Now let me verify the real server data — does RespinLockedPos actually contain positions in col0/5? Let me check the JSON and the decode logic.

input.command: grep -nE "DecodeServerPosByRowConfig|RowConfig|m_rowConfig" assets/Script/Common/Common.ts | head; echo "---"; grep -oE "RespinLockedPos[^]]*\]" .claude_temp/respin_and_freegame.json | head -5
input.description: Find decode logic and sample RespinLockedPos

content.content:
358:				const decoded = this.DecodeServerPosByRowConfig( n );
379:	static DecodeServerPosByRowConfig( data: number ): { col: number; row: number } | null
406:	static EncodeServerPosByRowConfig( col: number, row: number ): number
---
RespinLockedPos": [0, 1, 2, 3, 12, 20, 21, 22, 23]
RespinLockedPos": [6, 17]
RespinLockedPos": [0, 1, 2, 3, 7, 9, 12, 13, 19, 20, 21, 22, 23]
RespinLockedPos": [5, 6]
RespinLockedPos": [18]

content.text: The real server data confirms RespinLockedPos contains values 0,1,2,3 and 20,21,22,23. Let me verify the decode mapping to confirm these map to col0 and col5.

content.content:
273:    static COL = 6;
284:    static readonly ROW_CONFIG: readonly number[] = Game_Define.LEGACY_PLATE_LAYOUT

273	    static COL = 6;

277	     * - true = 1 代 server 過渡期 [5,4,4,4,4,5]（26 格）

content.text:
ROW_CONFIG = [4,4,4,4,4,4]. So decoding:
- pos 0-3 → col0 (acc 0, height 4)
- pos 4-7 → col1
- ...
- pos 20-23 → col5 (acc 20, height 4)

So the real data `[0,1,2,3, ... ,20,21,22,23]` indeed maps to col0 (0-3) and col5 (20-23). The finding's factual decode is correct.

Now the key question for the finding's severity: the finding claims the **committed** f2f739e version produces visual double-locking / overlay corruption on col0/5. Let me check whether col0/5 entries even reach the merge in the committed version — specifically, does `ResolveLockedEntries` filter out col0/5? Let me read that.

Confirmed: `ResolveLockedEntries` does NOT filter out col0/5 — it decodes all serverPos and pushes all valid entries. So col0/5 entries DO reach `MergeRespinLocks` and the `RespinLockedSet`. In the committed f2f739e version, the loop over `RespinLockedSet` had no col0/5 skip, so `CreateSymbolStaticNode` would run for col0/5 entries.

Now the critical question for whether this is a REAL problem: does the committed version actually cause visible corruption, or is the static node harmless? And more importantly — is this finding actionable given the fix is already in working tree? Let me verify the SetColumnLock behavior and CreateSymbolStaticNode to assess the "double lock / overlay corruption" severity claim.

Now I have a full picture. In the committed f2f739e version, for col0/5 entries:
1. `SetColumnLock` marks the column locked (reel won't spin, original symbol stays).
2. The loop also calls `HideSymbolByRow(row)` on the original cell AND `CreateSymbolStaticNode` to overlay a static node.

So in f2f739e there genuinely IS redundant work on col0/5 (hide original + overlay static). The finding correctly describes this. The working-tree fix skips the static node for col0/5.

Now the key assessment for the finding itself. The finding's own `fix` text states:
- The uncommitted switch handles this correctly (direction correct).
- `AssertLockedConsistency` remains consistent because both expected and actual include col0/5.
- The finding is essentially: (1) commit the fix, (2) fix the spec comment "only col1-4", (3) runtime verify.

Let me verify the one possibly-substantive correctness concern: whether the committed f2f739e version's overlay actually causes a *functional* bug vs cosmetic redundancy. And critically — whether the finding is really about the committed snapshot or the working tree (which already fixes it). Let me check AssertLockedConsistency exists.

input.description: Find consistency assert and merge in GameView

content.text:
There's no `AssertLockedConsistency` in GameView — the finding's `fix` text references it, but it may be elsewhere or hypothetical. That's a minor inaccuracy in the finding's reasoning but doesn't change the core claim. Let me do a final check on the actual severity: is the redundant static node on col0/5 actually visually wrong, or benign?

The reasoning: col0/5 with `SetColumnLock` keeps the reel symbol. Overlaying a static node on top (same symbol, same position) at LAYER.ONE would show the same symbol — visually likely identical or a harmless duplicate, UNLESS the static and the reel symbol differ (e.g. CollectAndRetrigger→Collect remapping). For a Collect symbol, the static would show "Collect" while reel shows "CollectAndRetrigger" — that IS a visible difference. So there is a plausible visual concern in the committed version.

Now let me assess the finding holistically. The finding is rated **medium**. Its substance:
1. Factual claims (RespinLockedPos contains col0/5, decode maps 0-3→col0 / 20-23→col5, committed f2f739e overlays static on col0/5) — all **verified true**.
2. The fix is already in working tree, correct direction.
3. Recommendations are reasonable (commit fix, correct spec comment, runtime verify).

The only way this is a false positive is if the review scope explicitly **includes** the uncommitted working-tree changes (it does — "審查範圍 = git diff bab1dbd~2 HEAD 的核心變更 + 未 commit 變更"). Since the working tree already fixes the issue, one could argue the problem is already resolved in the reviewed state. But the finding itself acknowledges this and reframes it as "the fix is uncommitted; commit f2f739e has the bug" plus a spec documentation defect.

Let me confirm the spec actually says "only col1-4" to validate recommendation #2.

13:| 鎖定位置來源 | client 掃 Cash(14)/Mystery(20) 推導（ScanRespinLockedPositions） | **server 直接給 `PlateInfo.RespinLockedPos`（增量 serverPos[]），client 跨手累加** | proto 變更：RespinLockedPos 從 RoundInfo 移到 PlateInfo（d.ts:1008/1033，🟢A 逐字驗證）；proto參數說明「Respin 時該盤面鎖住不重轉的位置」；用戶確認「增量」 |
14:| F-4 掃描 cursor off-by-one | 三案各有變體、需 m_respinLockedSet 防 | **大幅消失**：不掃盤面，只累加 server 給的位置；僅存「讀哪個 plate 的 RespinLockedPos」index 規格（§5.5） | 同上 |
28:4. **Respin 接通新協議**：鎖定改讀 server `PlateInfo.RespinLockedPos` 累加 + CheckState 優先序重排 + LeaveRespinState 補 ENTER_FREE 出口 + 刪死碼。
53:                    │     → ENTER_RESPIN（首手:設旗標+鎖col0/5+宣告; 累加鎖定）→ SPIN(plate k+1) ┘
82:| P-3 | `RespinLockedPos`（PlateInfo）= 該 plate 結束要**新增**鎖住的位置（serverPos[]，增量），只含 col 1~4 | 🔴 用戶確認 + proto參數說明「Respin 時該盤面鎖住不重轉的位置」🟡B 佐證 |

content.text:
This is decisive. The spec at line 82 (P-3) explicitly states `RespinLockedPos ... 只含 col 1~4` ("only contains col 1-4"), and line 233 says `col 0/5 不在 server serverPos（P-3，只給 col1-4）` ("col0/5 not in server serverPos, only gives col1-4"). The real server data directly contradicts this — RespinLockedPos contains 0,1,2,3 (col0) and 20,21,22,23 (col5).

So the finding's central claim — **real data violates spec P-3's "only col1-4" assumption** — is factually verified against both the spec text and the actual JSON.

Let me do a final sanity check that this isn't a `[5,4,4,4,4,5]` legacy-layout edge case where pos 0-4 could be col0 differently. LEGACY_PLATE_LAYOUT is `false`, so ROW_CONFIG = [4,4,4,4,4,4], confirming pos 0-3=col0, 20-23=col5.

I have enough to judge. Let me summarize my verification.

**Verification results:**

All core factual claims of the finding are confirmed:
1. **Spec P-3 assumes "only col1-4"** — VERIFIED (P1-design-spec.md:82 `只含 col 1~4`, :233 `只給 col1-4`).
2. **Real RespinLockedPos contains col0/5** — VERIFIED. JSON has `[0,1,2,3,...,20,21,22,23]`; ROW_CONFIG=[4,4,4,4,4,4] (LEGACY_PLATE_LAYOUT=false) decodes 0-3→col0, 20-23→col5.
3. **ResolveLockedEntries does NOT filter col0/5** — VERIFIED (RespinPlateLogic.ts decodes all serverPos, pushes all valid entries; col0/5 reach MergeRespinLocks/RespinLockedSet).
4. **Committed f2f739e overlays static nodes on col0/5** — VERIFIED (git show f2f739e: loop has no col0/5 skip; runs CreateSymbolStaticNode for all entries → redundant overlay while SetColumnLock already keeps reel symbol; CollectAndRetrigger→Collect remap means overlay can visually differ).
5. **Fix is uncommitted (working tree only)** — VERIFIED (git diff HEAD shows EXCLUDE_LOCK_COLUMNS_FROM_STATIC added but not committed; HEAD=81f1f76).

**Attempts to refute that failed:**
- Not a misread: the decode arithmetic and reel-config are exactly as the finding states.
- Not handled elsewhere: the pure layer does not filter col0/5; only the uncommitted switch does.
- It IS in the review's execution path: review scope explicitly includes both committed range AND uncommitted changes.

**Minor inaccuracies in finding (don't invalidate it):**
- References `AssertLockedConsistency` which doesn't exist by that name (the spec calls it a fail-loud 斷言 at §5.7/line 239); the consistency reasoning is still directionally sound.
- The "視覺上雙重鎖定/可能蓋圖錯亂" is asserted as a runtime symptom but is partly inferred (correctly flagged by the finding itself as needing runtime confirmation in recommendation #3).

The finding is a **real, accurate documentation/spec-vs-reality defect** plus a correctly-identified redundant-overlay in the committed snapshot. Severity medium is appropriate: the spec carries a false premise that will mislead maintainers, and the fix exists but is uncommitted. The recommendations (commit the fix, correct the spec comment) are valid and actionable.

input.reasoning:
反駁失敗，finding 為真問題。逐項查證全部成立：

1. 規格 P-3 確實假設「只含 col1-4」：P1-design-spec.md:82 `RespinLockedPos ... 只含 col 1~4`、:233 `col 0/5 不在 server serverPos（P-3，只給 col1-4）`。

2. 真實回包牴觸此前提：.claude_temp/respin_and_freegame.json 的 RespinLockedPos 含 [0,1,2,3,...,20,21,22,23]。Game_Define.LEGACY_PLATE_LAYOUT=false → ROW_CONFIG=[4,4,4,4,4,4]，Common.DecodeServerPosByRowConfig 累加解碼：pos 0-3→col0、20-23→col5。decode 算術與 finding 描述一致。

3. ResolveLockedEntries（RespinPlateLogic.ts）不過濾 col0/5——對所有 serverPos 解碼並 push 有效 entry，故 col0/5 進入 MergeRespinLocks / RespinLockedSet。

4. 已 commit 的 f2f739e（git show 驗證）HandleReel 迴圈無 col0/5 略過，對全集合（含 col0/5）跑 HideSymbolByRow + CreateSymbolStaticNode，而 SetColumnLock 已讓 reel 留存原符號 → col0/5 既整列鎖又疊靜態張，屬冗餘；CollectAndRetrigger→Collect 重映使疊圖可能與 reel 符號不同，有可見差異風險。

5. 修正（EXCLUDE_LOCK_COLUMNS_FROM_STATIC=true）確實只在 working tree（git diff HEAD），未 commit；HEAD=81f1f76。

反駁路徑皆封死：非誤讀（decode/config 完全吻合）、非別處已處理（純層不過濾，只有未 commit 開關處理）、在審查執行路徑內（審查範圍明列含未 commit 變更）。修正方向正確且 finding 自承「停在 working tree 未提交」。

降低信心的次要瑕疵：finding 引用 AssertLockedConsistency 此命名在 GameView 不存在（規格 §5.7/line239 稱 fail-loud 斷言），且「蓋圖錯亂」屬部分推論（finding 自己已標註需執行期確認）。但這些不影響核心 spec-vs-reality 矛盾與冗餘疊圖的事實。建議（commit 修正、更正規格註解）有效可行。severity medium 合理。

content.text:
驗證完成。Finding 為真問題（isReal=true，confidence 0.85）。

核心事實全部查證成立：
- 規格 P-3 假設「只含 col1-4」：`P1-design-spec.md:82, :233`
- 真實回包 RespinLockedPos 含 col0/5：JSON `[0,1,2,3,...,20,21,22,23]`，ROW_CONFIG=[4,4,4,4,4,4] 解碼 0-3→col0、20-23→col5
- `ResolveLockedEntries`（RespinPlateLogic.ts）不過濾 col0/5
- 已 commit 的 f2f739e 對全集合疊靜態張（冗餘）
- 修正開關 EXCLUDE_LOCK_COLUMNS_FROM_STATIC 確實只在 working tree 未 commit

反駁三路徑（誤讀／別處已處理／不在執行路徑）皆封死。次要瑕疵：finding 引用的 `AssertLockedConsistency` 命名在 GameView 不存在（實為規格 §5.7 的 fail-loud 斷言），但不動搖核心 spec-vs-reality 矛盾。
